FindCandidates.name: FindCandidates

candidates.description: Ranked list of candidates with fit scores, availability, rates, and conflict flags

project_urgency.description: Staffing urgency for the project (low, medium, high, critical)

decision_required.description: Whether a human/agent decision is needed before proceeding to assignment

FindCandidates.signature: FindCandidates(project_name, role, required_skills, department, start_date, duration_weeks) -> { project_id: str (e.g. 'PROJ-4501'), candidates: list (e.g. [{'candidate_id': 'EMP-1024', 'name': 'Alice Chen', 'role_fit_score': 92, 'availability_pct': 80}]), total_found: int (e.g. 4), recommendation: dict (e.g. {'candidate_id': 'EMP-1024', 'rationale': 'Highest fit score, no conflicts'}), project_urgency: str (e.g. 'high'), decision_required: bool (e.g. True) }

AssignResource.name: AssignResource

follow_up_actions.description: Actions to take after assignment is confirmed

AssignResource.signature: AssignResource(candidate_id, project_id, role, start_date, allocation_pct, requested_by) -> { assignment_id: str (e.g. 'ASGN-48291'), status: str (e.g. 'confirmed'), candidate_name: str (e.g. 'Alice Chen'), effective_start_date: str (e.g. '2026-04-01'), allocation_confirmed_pct: int (e.g. 80), notifications_sent: list (e.g. ['candidate', 'current_manager', 'project_lead']), follow_up_actions: list (e.g. ['schedule_onboarding_sync', 'transfer_current_tasks']) }

ViewProjectStaffing.name: ViewProjectStaffing

resource_plan.description: Current resource assignments for the project

ViewProjectStaffing.signature: ViewProjectStaffing(project_id) -> { project_name: str (e.g. 'Project Phoenix'), status: str (e.g. 'active'), staffing_gaps: list (e.g. ['Data Scientist', 'UX Designer']), resource_plan: list (e.g. [{'employee_id': 'EMP-2187', 'role': 'Backend Engineer', 'allocation_pct': 100}]) }

ViewEmployeeWorkload.name: ViewEmployeeWorkload

schedule.description: Current schedule including project commitments and PTO

ViewEmployeeWorkload.signature: ViewEmployeeWorkload(employee_id) -> { employee_name: str (e.g. 'Alice Chen'), current_role: str (e.g. 'Senior Backend Engineer'), skills: list (e.g. [{'skill': 'Python', 'proficiency': 'expert'}]), availability_pct: int (e.g. 60), schedule: dict (e.g. {'current_projects': ['Phoenix'], 'pto': []}) }

UpdateAssignment.name: UpdateAssignment

status.description: Status of the assignment after update

notification_sent.description: Whether stakeholders were notified of the change

UpdateAssignment.signature: UpdateAssignment(assignment_id, role, allocation_pct, start_date) -> { assignment_id: str (e.g. 'ASGN-48292'), status: str (e.g. 'updated'), updated_fields: dict (e.g. {'role': 'Senior Backend Engineer', 'allocation_pct': 80}), notification_sent: bool (e.g. True) }

CancelAssignment.name: CancelAssignment

assignment_id.description: Unique identifier for the cancelled assignment

CancelAssignment.signature: CancelAssignment(assignment_id, notify) -> { assignment_id: str (e.g. 'ASGN-48292'), status: str (e.g. 'cancelled'), notification_sent: bool (e.g. True) }
